name: Gorafe Desert Route
name_original: Desierto de Gorafe – circular por Fuente del Carrizal y Puntal de Pau Diego
description: Hike 6.8 miles (10.9 km) through the Gorafe Desert's colorful badlands to El Puntal de Pau Diego for panoramic views in the Granada Geopark.
region_intro: Hiking around Gorafe offers access to a distinctive badlands landscape within the Guadix-Baza Depression. This region is characterized by rolling hills, canyons, ravines, and "fairy chimneys" formed by erosion, creating a unique geological environment. The terrain features a color palette ranging from white to ochre and vibrant reds, depending on mineral composition. Additionally, the area is home to the Gorafe Megalithic Park, containing more than 240 dolmens. Best hiking trails around Gorafe The most popular hiking route is Desierto de Gorafe – circular to Los Coloraos y Cerro de la Bandera, a 14.9 miles (23.9 km) trail that takes 6 hours 42 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the dramatic red badlands and offers panoramic views. Another top favourite among local hikers is Gorafe Desert Route, a moderate 6.8 miles (10.9 km) path. This trail leads through the colorful badlands to El Puntal de Pau Diego for expansive views. Local hikers also love the Badlands of Granada Geopark loop from SL-A 278 Cañada de la Ventanilla - Campillos, a 5.8 miles (9.3 km) trail leading through the Geopark's unique formations, often completed in about 2 hours 35 minutes. Hiking around Gorafe is defined by its dramatic badlands, extensive gullies, and ancient dolmens. The network of routes offers options for various ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging long-distance trails. The routes in Gorafe are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from nearly 100 reviews. More than 400 hikers have used komoot to explore Gorafe's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there in Gorafe?
A: Gorafe offers a diverse network of nearly 20 hiking trails. These routes cater to various difficulty levels, from easy strolls to challenging long-distance treks, all set within the region's unique badlands landscape.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Gorafe?
A: Yes, Gorafe has a few easy hiking trails perfect for beginners or those seeking a relaxed walk. While the majority are moderate, you can find routes that allow you to experience the unique scenery without significant challenge.

Q: What kind of unique landscapes or geological features can I expect to see?
A: Gorafe is renowned for its dramatic 'badlands' landscape, characterized by rolling hills, canyons, ravines, and 'fairy chimneys' carved by erosion. You'll encounter a striking color palette ranging from white to ochre and vibrant reds. A particularly famous area is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5459236" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Coloraos Badlands&lt;/a&gt;, known for its predominant red hues and stunning sandstone formations.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Gorafe?
A: Yes, many of Gorafe's trails are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14622958" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Badlands of Granada Geopark loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 9.3 km trail, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31682391" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorafe Megalithic Park – Dolmen del Conquín loop&lt;/a&gt; offers a 10.7 km moderate hike.

Q: What historical or archaeological sites can I visit while hiking in Gorafe?
A: Gorafe is home to the significant Gorafe Megalithic Park, which features over 240 dolmens (prehistoric stone monuments). These ancient sites are spread along marked trails, offering a fascinating glimpse into ancient civilizations. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31682391" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorafe Megalithic Park – Dolmen del Conquín loop&lt;/a&gt; is a great way to explore some of these monuments.

Q: Where can I find the best panoramic views in Gorafe?
A: For breathtaking panoramic views of the desert landscape, consider routes that lead to elevated viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983200260/gorafe-desert-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorafe Desert Route&lt;/a&gt; takes you to El Puntal de Pau Diego for expansive vistas. Other notable viewpoints in the region include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5520220" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puntal de Don Diego Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, Bácor Point Viewpoint, and Campillos Viewpoint.

Q: Is there parking available at the trailheads in Gorafe?
A: While specific parking facilities at every trailhead may vary, many routes in Gorafe are accessible by car, and you can typically find informal parking areas near the start of popular trails. Given the rural nature of the region, driving is generally recommended for accessing the various hiking spots.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Gorafe?
A: The best time to hike in Gorafe is during the cooler months, from autumn through spring (October to May). Summers can be extremely hot, making hiking challenging. Spring offers pleasant temperatures and potential for some greenery, while autumn provides comfortable conditions and beautiful light for photography.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Gorafe?
A: The trails in Gorafe are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from nearly 100 reviews. Hikers often praise the unique, otherworldly badlands scenery, the sense of tranquility, and the well-preserved archaeological sites like the dolmens.

Q: Are there any shorter, moderate hikes that still offer great views of the badlands?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983200260/gorafe-desert-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorafe Desert Route&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 10.9 km path that leads through the colorful badlands to El Puntal de Pau Diego, providing excellent panoramic views. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38222418" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorafe Desert Canyons – Los Coloraos Badlands loop&lt;/a&gt;, an 8 km moderate hike that immerses you in the dramatic red landscape.

Q: Can I explore the famous Los Coloraos area on a hike?
A: Yes, Los Coloraos, known for its vibrant red badlands, is a highlight of the Gorafe region and can be explored on foot. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983202252/desierto-de-gorafe-circular-to-los-coloraos-y-cerro-de-la-bandera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desierto de Gorafe – circular to Los Coloraos y Cerro de la Bandera&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 23.9 km route that takes you deep into this dramatic landscape, offering incredible views.

Q: What should I know about the terrain when hiking in Gorafe?
A: The terrain in Gorafe is characterized by its 'badlands' geology, meaning you'll encounter varied surfaces including loose earth, gravel, and eroded sandstone. Trails often traverse rolling hills, canyons, and ravines. Sturdy hiking boots with good ankle support and grip are highly recommended due to the uneven and sometimes slippery ground, especially after rain.
tips: If you're here early in the morning or late in the evening, use the viewpoint to capture the array of colours and shapes laid out before you.

The Fountain trail is an offshoot to a a tiny spring. Be aware that this trail is a dead end, and that it’s likely that there will not be drinkable water when you arrive. 
When we were there in mid April it was a mud hole teeming with frogs and frogspawn.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this hike?
faqs.answer: This moderate circular hike usually takes around 3 hours and 6 minutes to complete, covering a distance of approximately 10.9 kilometers.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail traverses the unique 'badlands' of the Gorafe Desert, characterized by rolling hills and intricate rock formations sculpted by erosion. You'll experience a surreal, almost lunar landscape with striking rock colors ranging from white to ochre and red, particularly in the vibrant 'Los Coloraos' areas.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Key highlights include the stunning panoramic views from El Puntal de Pau Diego, which offers breathtaking vistas of the canyon landscape. You'll also pass Fuente del Carrizal, a small spring, though it's not a reliable source of drinking water.

faqs.question: Is the Desierto de Gorafe – circular by Fuente del Carrizal y Puntal de Pau Diego trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Rated as moderate, the trail requires good fitness due to its length and elevation gain of approximately 250 meters. While paths are generally accessible, it's not ideal for absolute beginners without some hiking experience.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike in the Gorafe Desert?
faqs.answer: The best time to visit is during the cooler months of spring (March-May) and autumn (September-November) to avoid the intense summer heat. Winters can be cold, but often offer clear, crisp hiking conditions.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally welcome on this trail. However, always keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure their safety, especially given the exposed terrain and potential for other hikers.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for the Granada Geopark?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike this trail within the Granada Geopark. The park is an open natural area, but always respect the environment and local regulations.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the lack of shade and intense sun, it's crucial to bring plenty of water, wear a hat, and use sunscreen. Sturdy hiking shoes are recommended for the varied terrain. Snacks and a first-aid kit are also advisable.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares sections with several other trails, including the Ruta del Desierto de Gorafe, Gorafe - Vereda Chirlata, and El Camino Espiritual del Sur. You might also encounter parts of the Cañada de la Ventanilla-Campillos and SL-A 278 Cañada de la Ventanilla - Campillos.

faqs.question: Is there reliable drinking water available along the trail?
faqs.answer: No, you should not rely on finding drinkable water on the trail. While the route passes Fuente del Carrizal, it is often muddy and not a safe source for hydration. Always carry sufficient water for your entire hike.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Specific parking information for this exact trailhead is not detailed, but generally, trails in the Gorafe Desert area have designated parking spots near their starting points. It's recommended to check the Komoot tour map for the precise starting location and any nearby parking facilities before you go.
